sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Good! Transformation happened without game over (player was on RIGHT). After transformation: 14 green blocks, 7 purple objects. Clicking purple objects did nothing. Need to figure out post-transformation goal. Let me try clicking just ONE green block closest to player to test if it's safe.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: player at (38, 50), 14 green blocks scattered
# Try clicking green block closest to player: (33, 51)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 51}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
by_color = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    by_color.setdefault(n['color'], []).append(n)

green = by_color.get('N', [])
print(f"After clicking (33,51): Green={len(green)}, GameOver={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

# Check what changed
if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    print("Board changed!")
    for color, nodes in by_color.items():
        if color not in ['B', 'g', 'G']:
            positions = []
            for n in nodes:
                boundary = n['boundary']
                rows = [pt[0] for pt in boundary]
                cols = [pt[1] for pt in boundary]
                center_row = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
                center_col = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
                positions.append(f"({center_row},{center_col})")
            print(f"  {color}: {len(nodes)} objects, pos={positions}")
else:
    print("Board did not change")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: player at (38, 50), 14 green blocks scattered
# Try clicking green block closest to player: (33, 51)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 51}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
by_color = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    by_color.setdefault(n['color'], []).append(n)

green = by_color.get('N', [])
print(f"After clicking (33,51): Green={len(green)}, GameOver={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

# Check what changed
if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    print("Board changed!")
    for color, nodes in by_color.items():
        if color not in ['B', 'g', 'G']:
            positions = []
            for n in nodes:
                boundary = n['boundary']
                rows = [pt[0] for pt in boundary]
                cols = [pt[1] for pt in boundary]
                center_row = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
                center_col = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
                positions.append(f"({center_row},{center_col})")
            print(f"  {color}: {len(nodes)} objects, pos={positions}")
else:
    print("Board did not change")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
